title: Apex-tunable dials forward-seam memo
summary: Analysis memo (NOT a plan) preserving the apex-tunable-dials seam without speculative infrastructure. Ranks 3 dial candidates, splits arbitration into already-supports vs needs-extending, defers the Console 'dial values' widget to a generic 'Active Policies' panel, and surfaces canon-promotion failure modes for indie operators inheriting org-tuned dials. No actor ships dial code from this deliberation; operator reviews at next session.

body.scope_note: This atom is analysis, not a strategic commitment. NOT a plan. NO actor ships dial code. NO new policy atoms. NO Console PR. Any subsequent canon-shape edit this memo eventually motivates routes through dev-canon-proposals-via-cto-not-direct (separate CTO plan + operator /decide).

1_autonomy_tier.current_encoding: Ad-hoc temporary elevation atoms (e.g. pol-cto-temp-self-approve-2026-04-26-08h with matching scheduled revert).
1_autonomy_tier.future_shape: Finite enum {off, draft-only, draft-and-dispatch, merge-allowed} per principal scope.
1_autonomy_tier.why_first: The elevation atom IS the proto-dial; operators are already manually tuning it via timed atoms. Demand is concrete and recurring.

2_per_pr_cost_cap.future_shape: Tier per principal-trust level; orgs running 50 actors will want differentiated caps.
2_per_pr_cost_cap.why_second: Atom is the natural carrier; demand scales with actor population, not yet pressing for indies.

3_circuit_breaker_threshold_window.current_encoding: pol-actor-message-circuit-breaker already exposes denial_count_trip_threshold and window_ms as canon-tunable.
3_circuit_breaker_threshold_window.future_shape: Already shaped for the dial pattern; no structural change needed.
3_circuit_breaker_threshold_window.why_third: Tuning demand is bounded by actual breaker trips; surfaces last because most deployments will not hit the breaker often enough to want differentiation.

b_arbitration_already_supports_vs_needs_.already_supports: Apex sets default at top scope; sub-scopes override with higher-priority atoms; arbitration resolves the chain via the principal hierarchy and scope/priority machinery. No new substrate primitive needed for dial values per se.
b_arbitration_already_supports_vs_needs_.needs_extending_when_first_dial_lands: A discoverability hint (e.g. dial_id field or is_dial: true marker) so consumers can enumerate dials without grepping policy atoms by name. NOT NEEDED today; introducing it speculatively would foreclose the seam by hardcoding dial-vs-policy as a substrate distinction.

c_console_dial_values_widget_today.forward_compat_path: Surface the existing active-elevations placeholder (the in-flight Console control-panel widget cited via dev-apex-tunable-dials-future-seam) as a generic 'Active Policies' read-only panel that lists priority-resolved policy atoms in scope. When dials land, dial-tagged atoms render with extra UX without inventing the dial concept speculatively.
c_console_dial_values_widget_today.why: Per dev-apex-tunable-dials-future-seam (4) and dev-extreme-rigor-and-research, naming a UX before the second concrete dial exists is the over-engineering this directive prohibits.

surprise_strict.failure: Indie pulls a canon bundle whose org-scope dial overrides the bootstrap default; PR suddenly blocked.
surprise_strict.mitigation: Bootstrap canon writes indie-floor defaults to LOCAL scope at lag init; org canon promotion writes at HIGHER scope, but local-scope atoms always win for the local principal in arbitration order. Promotion never overwrites local atoms; it adds higher-scope atoms whose local override is one /decide away.

silent_behavior_change_on_upgrade.failure: Existing deployment posture changes silently when a new dial ships.
silent_behavior_change_on_upgrade.mitigation: Per dev-apex-tunable-dials-future-seam (5) every dial ships with a default matching pre-dial behavior; existing deployments do not change posture on upgrade.

indie_discoverability.failure: Without a Console panel, an indie cannot tell why their PR is blocked ('oh no, suddenly security=strict, why am I being told my PR can't merge for 7 days').
indie_discoverability.mitigation: The 'Active Policies' panel from (c) makes the limiting atom visible with its source scope; indie sees both the rule and where it came from.

promotion_time_canon_drift.failure: Org canon bundle proposes a dial change that mutates runtime posture without human ratification.
promotion_time_canon_drift.mitigation: Per dev-canon-proposals-via-cto-not-direct, any canon-shape edit ratifies via CTO plan + operator /decide, not silent runtime injection.

body.closing: This memo preserves the seam without building it. If the first concrete dial lands and contradicts the ranking, this atom is superseded additively by a revision atom citing it, not reversed.
